disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive confusion and altered behaviour developing over the past 48 hours in a person with known advanced liver disease
trigger_question_examples: What has changed over the last day or two?
How has his thinking or behaviour been?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Over the last two days he's got more and more muddled and confused, and today he's hardly making sense at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked reduction in level of consciousness — deeply drowsy, only rousable to loud voice or shaking
trigger_question_examples: Can you wake him up? How much does he respond?
Is he able to stay awake and talk to you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's so sleepy I can barely wake him — he only stirs if I shout his name or shake his shoulder, then drifts straight back off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reversed sleep-wake pattern — awake all night, profoundly sleepy through the day — building over the past week
trigger_question_examples: How has his sleep been?
Is he awake in the day or the night?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: For the past week he's been up all night and then asleep all day — it's completely back to front.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Coarse flapping tremor of the outstretched hands with slurred speech
trigger_question_examples: What are his hands doing?
Is his speech clear or slurred?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When he puts his hands out they shake and flap about, and his words are all slurred and mumbled.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No bowel motion for about 4 days (constipation)
trigger_question_examples: When did he last open his bowels?
Has he been constipated?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He hasn't been to the toilet properly in about four days — he's really constipated.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Stopped the prescribed lactulose syrup 4 days ago because of the taste
trigger_question_examples: Is he taking all his usual medicines?
Has he missed any of his liver medicine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's refused his sweet syrup medicine for the last four days — he can't stand the taste, so the bottle's still full.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known advanced liver scarring from past heavy alcohol use, now abstinent (established baseline condition)
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any ongoing medical conditions?
Tell me about his liver problem.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's got bad liver scarring from years of drinking — he's off the alcohol now but the liver damage stayed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Caregiver has not seen any vomiting of blood or black tarry stools
trigger_question_examples: Has he vomited anything, especially blood or coffee-ground material?
Have his stools been black or tarry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't seen him bring up any blood and I haven't noticed any black tarry poo — but he has been so blocked up.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I've been so focused on how sleepy he is, I haven't really checked.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, shakes or chills noticed by the caregiver
trigger_question_examples: Has he felt hot or had a fever?
Any shivering or shakes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He hasn't felt hot to me and I haven't seen him shivering.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No recent fall or head injury, and no one-sided weakness of the face or limbs noticed
trigger_question_examples: Has he had any fall or knock to the head recently?
Have you noticed any weakness on one side of his body or face?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, he hasn't fallen or banged his head that I know of, and I haven't seen one side of him go weak — it's his whole body that's just floppy and sleepy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: So drowsy he is at risk of choking — struggles to swallow safely when this sleepy
trigger_question_examples: Is he able to swallow safely?
Are you worried about him choking when he's this sleepy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When he's this out of it I'm scared he'll choke — he can barely swallow and just splutters if I try to give him anything.
